title: Securing Express API Routes with Rate Limiting

statement.markdown: Implement rate limiting for an Express API to prevent abuse. The API has two endpoints: `/users` and `/products`. The `/users` endpoint should allow 10 requests per minute, while the `/products` endpoint should allow 5 requests per minute. If the rate limit is exceeded, return a `429 Too Many Requests` error with a custom error message. The API uses the `express-rate-limit` library.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a string representing the API endpoint (either `/users` or `/products`).
statement.outputFormat: The output will be a string representing the rate-limited API endpoint.
statement.notes: Note that the rate limit should be enforced globally, meaning that all requests to the API should be subject to the rate limit.

examples.output: Rate limit exceeded for /users: 10 requests per minute
examples.explanation: Exceeds the rate limit of 10 requests per minute

examples.output: Rate limit exceeded for /products: 5 requests per minute
examples.explanation: Exceeds the rate limit of 5 requests per minute

hints.text: Use the `express-rate-limit` library to implement rate limiting.

editorial.title: Implementing Rate Limiting with Express Rate Limit
editorial.intuition: The `express-rate-limit` library provides an easy-to-use API for implementing rate limiting. To use it, create a new instance of the `RateLimiterMiddleware` class, passing in the desired rate limit and window. Then, apply the middleware to the Express app using the `use` method.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible approach is to use a brute-force approach, where you manually track the number of requests made to each endpoint and return an error if the rate limit is exceeded.
editorial.betterSolution: A better approach is to use the `express-rate-limit` library, which provides a more efficient and scalable way to implement rate limiting.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use the `express-rate-limit` library and configure it to enforce the rate limit globally, affecting all requests to the API.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: To prove that the solution is correct, you can use a testing framework to simulate requests to the API and verify that the rate limit is enforced correctly.
editorial.dryRun: Here is a step-by-step example of how to implement rate limiting using the `express-rate-limit` library:
commonMistakes: Forgetting to configure the rate limit globally

testCases.output: Rate limit exceeded for /users: 10 requests per minute
Rate limit exceeded for /products: 5 requests per minute
